steps.title: Recognize the restatement
steps.content: Immediately after “negative words saw a faster rate of cognate replacement,” the passage restates this using plainer language: “the rate at which a word will be replaced over time with a noncognate form.” These two phrases describe the same concept.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content:
“That is,” (“in other words”) marks a restatement or clarification, which is exactly what's needed when a technical term is being defined in plainer language.

The others either suggest similarity (“likewise,”), introduce a specific example (“for example,”), or add new information (“in addition,”) rather than clarifying the term.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
